# Decisions: team-readiness

- 2026-07-12T07:31:52.000Z — chose **Proceed with identity (envelope user field) and team docs under the user-directed team-readiness initiative — the CLAUDE.md "no team/identity features" guard-rail was scope law for the Fable build window (Jul 3–7) and is explicitly superseded by the user's direction for this initiative; the untouched remainder (no UI, no sync service, no telemetry, schema confined to packages/schema + projection templates) stays in force** over refusing the initiative as a guard-rail violation, or silently ignoring the guard-rail without a record because the guard-rail's own escape hatch requires logging the conflict and surfacing it; the user's explicit instruction is the authority the guard-rail defers to, and the initiative's own guard-rails (no sync-service code, no envelope-breaking changes) keep the original intent intact
- 2026-07-12T07:38:38.892Z — chose **T1 identity as an additive OPTIONAL envelope `user` field on v1 — stamped in makeEvent (the single local minting seam) from `git config user.email`, process-cached, omitted on any failure so an append can never fail on identity; validation accepts absence and rejects only present-but-malformed values; `sofar import` appends pre-minted envelopes and therefore never restamps** over an envelope v2 with a required user field, and retrofitting identity onto existing events because v2 would break the frozen envelope contract and force migration for zero gain over an optional field every reader already tolerates; retrofit is IMPOSSIBLE — authorship of already-appended events is unknowable after the fact (events are immutable and identity exists only at minting time), which is also why the field ships now, before first team adoption, rather than later
- 2026-07-12T07:44:00.345Z — chose **T2 scope includes `sofar uninit`: it now strips the exact union-merge line init installs (byte-match only; a customized events.jsonl rule is user content and stays), with --purge deleting the file when the removal empties it; presence detection in init is "any line whose first token is .sofar/**/events.jsonl" so a user-customized rule wins over ours** over touching only init as the task listed, leaving uninit ignorant of .gitattributes; and exact-line presence detection in init that would append our rule below a user's conflicting one because SPEC's uninit law is "exact inverse of init" and the Phase 8 acceptance round-trips (fresh-repo purge → only .git remains; init→uninit→init ≡ single init) fail byte-clean without the inverse; theirs-wins matches the .mcp.json customized-entry precedent; the phase-8 scenario-(b) fixture gained a pre-existing .gitattributes exactly as it already carries every other init-managed file
